_source: IPCC AR6 WGII (vulnerable groups), UNDRR Sendai Framework (at-risk populations), EDF equity framework (Shi 2024 rubric), and existing case library demographic analysis
_note: Controlled vocabulary for population groups facing disproportionate climate vulnerability. Groups are socially-defined (not geographic) to support equity analysis across justice dimensions. Used to bind previously free-text affected_group fields. Sprint 5 addition — designed for EDF/Cornell AI equity impact study.

populations.name: Low-Income Households
populations.description: Households below poverty line or in lowest income quintiles; limited financial buffers for adaptation or recovery

populations.name: Renters / Non-Homeowners
populations.description: Renters and non-homeowners who lack control over housing upgrades, face displacement risk, and may be excluded from property-tied adaptation programs
populations._note: Documented in EDF equity framing as a distinct group from low-income; renters are vulnerable even when not poor

populations.name: Informal Settlement Residents
populations.description: Residents of informal, unplanned, or self-built settlements with insecure tenure, limited services, and high physical exposure

populations.name: Elderly / Older Adults
populations.description: Adults 65+ with elevated health vulnerability to extreme heat, cold, and disasters; may have mobility limitations and social isolation

populations.name: Children and Youth
populations.description: Children and young people (under 18) with heightened physiological sensitivity to heat and air quality and long time horizons for climate impact

populations.name: People with Disabilities
populations.description: Persons with physical, cognitive, or sensory disabilities facing accessibility barriers in evacuation, early-warning systems, and recovery programs

populations.name: Outdoor Workers
populations.description: Agricultural workers, construction workers, delivery workers, and others with occupational heat and weather exposure; often low-wage and without benefits

populations.name: Indigenous and Tribal Communities
populations.description: Indigenous peoples with place-based climate vulnerability, land rights issues, and cultural practices at risk; recognized in IPCC AR6 and Sendai as a priority group

populations.name: Racial / Ethnic Minorities
populations.description: Communities of color and ethnic minorities facing compounded climate and social vulnerabilities rooted in historical discrimination and structural inequity
populations._note: Particularly relevant in US context for EDF/Cornell study; IPCC AR6 Chapter 8 documents intersection of race and climate vulnerability

populations.name: Migrants and Refugees
populations.description: Internal and international migrants, asylum seekers, and refugees with limited legal status, social networks, and access to services

populations.name: Limited English Proficiency / Non-English Speakers
populations.description: Individuals with limited English proficiency who face barriers to accessing early warnings, services, and participation in planning processes
populations._note: Critical for procedural and epistemic justice dimensions; early-warning systems often fail this group

populations.name: Single-Parent Households
populations.description: Single-parent families with constrained financial and time resources for adaptation; predominantly headed by women

populations.name: Public Housing Residents
populations.description: Residents of public or subsidized housing with concentrated exposure and limited individual control over adaptation investments

populations.name: Unhoused / Houseless Populations
populations.description: Persons experiencing homelessness with maximum physical exposure to extreme weather events and minimal adaptive capacity

populations.name: Small-Scale / Subsistence Farmers
populations.description: Smallholder and subsistence farmers with direct livelihood exposure to climate hazards and limited resources for adaptation

populations.name: Women and Girls
populations.description: Women and girls facing gender-differentiated climate vulnerability through unequal resource access, gendered domestic roles, and exclusion from decision-making
populations._note: Use when source document explicitly documents gender-differentiated impacts; avoid when gender is not a documented dimension

populations.name: Medically Vulnerable Persons
populations.description: Persons with chronic illness, immunocompromised conditions, or medical equipment dependency with elevated health risk from power outages, heat, and air quality events

populations.name: Frontline Environmental Justice Communities
populations.description: Communities bearing a disproportionate cumulative environmental burden (pollution, hazard exposure, underinvestment) — aggregate term for EJ screening contexts. Use when source uses EJ framing explicitly.
populations._note: Aggregate EJ term; prefer more specific groups when documented. Aligns with EPA EJScreen, US Justice40 criteria, and EDF equity framing.
